qa.q: Who are the four gospel writers?
qa.a: Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John

qa.q: What is the Sermon on the Mount?
qa.a: Jesus's teachings recorded in Matthew 5-7, including the Beatitudes

qa.q: How many apostles did Jesus choose?
qa.a: Twelve apostles

qa.q: What is the Great Commission?
qa.a: Jesus's command to make disciples of all nations (Matthew 28:19-20)

qa.q: What are the Beatitudes?
qa.a: Eight blessings pronounced by Jesus at the start of the Sermon on the Mount

qa.q: Who wrote the Gospel of Mark?
qa.a: John Mark, often understood to be Peter's associate

qa.q: What is Mark's main emphasis?
qa.a: Jesus's actions and power, presenting Him as the Son of God

qa.q: What does 'Gospel' mean?
qa.a: Good news or good message

qa.q: How many times does Mark describe Jesus calming storms?
qa.a: Once (Mark 4:35-41)

qa.q: What is the 'messianic secret' in Mark?
qa.a: Jesus's repeated commands to keep His identity secret

qa.q: Who was Luke?
qa.a: A physician and companion of the Apostle Paul

qa.q: What unique parables does Luke include?
qa.a: The Good Samaritan and the Prodigal Son

qa.q: What is Luke's focus?
qa.a: Jesus's compassion for the poor and marginalized

qa.q: How many chapters does Luke have?
qa.a: 24 chapters

qa.q: What does Luke emphasize about the Holy Spirit?
qa.a: The role of the Holy Spirit in Jesus's ministry and the early church

qa.q: What is John's distinctive opening?
qa.a: 'In the beginning was the Word' - presenting Jesus as God's eternal Word

qa.q: How many 'signs' does John record?
qa.a: Seven major miracles or signs

qa.q: What is John's main theme?
qa.a: Jesus is the Son of God and believers can have eternal life through Him

qa.q: What is the Last Supper called in John?
qa.a: The washing of feet and the giving of the new commandment

qa.q: What are Jesus's 'I am' statements in John?
qa.a: Declarations of Jesus's identity (I am the bread, light, door, shepherd, etc.)

qa.q: What does Acts record?
qa.a: The history of the early Christian church after Jesus's ascension

qa.q: Who is the author of Acts?
qa.a: Luke, the gospel writer

qa.q: What is Pentecost?
qa.a: The day the Holy Spirit came upon the apostles and the church began

qa.q: What was Peter's role in Acts?
qa.a: He was a leading apostle and missionary to the Jewish people

qa.q: Who was Stephen?
qa.a: The first Christian martyr (deacon and evangelist)

qa.q: To whom did Paul write Romans?
qa.a: To the church at Rome

qa.q: What is the main theme of Romans?
qa.a: Salvation through faith in Jesus Christ

qa.q: What does Romans 3:23 say?
qa.a: 'All have sinned and fall short of the glory of God'

qa.q: What is justification by faith?
qa.a: The doctrine that believers are made right with God through faith, not works

qa.q: What is Paul's view on the law?
qa.a: The law showed sin but cannot save; salvation comes through Christ

qa.q: What problems did Paul address in Corinth?
qa.a: Division, sexual immorality, disputes, and misuse of spiritual gifts

qa.q: What is Paul's teaching on spiritual gifts?
qa.a: All believers receive gifts from the Holy Spirit to serve the church

qa.q: What is 1 Corinthians 13?
qa.a: Paul's description of love as the greatest spiritual gift

qa.q: What did Paul teach about the Lord's Supper?
qa.a: Instructions on proper observance and remembrance of Jesus's death

qa.q: What is the 'body of Christ'?
qa.a: A metaphor for the church where Christ is the head and believers are members

qa.q: What is Paul's main concern in 2 Corinthians?
qa.a: Defending his apostolic authority and reconciling with the church

qa.q: What does Paul say about suffering?
qa.a: 'When I am weak, then I am strong' - strength through weakness

qa.q: What is 'the thorn in Paul's flesh'?
qa.a: An affliction Paul prayed would be removed (likely a physical ailment)

qa.q: What does Paul teach about giving?
qa.a: Generous giving should be cheerful and voluntary

qa.q: What is the 'new covenant'?
qa.a: God's promise of salvation through Christ, replacing the old law

qa.q: What is the main issue in Galatians?
qa.a: Whether Gentiles must follow Jewish law to be saved

qa.q: What is Paul's central argument?
qa.a: Salvation is by faith in Christ alone, not by works of the law

qa.q: What does Paul mean by 'Christian freedom'?
qa.a: Freedom from the law through faith in Christ

qa.q: What are the 'works of the flesh' Paul lists?
qa.a: Sins like immorality, idolatry, hatred, and envy

qa.q: What is the 'fruit of the Spirit'?
qa.a: Love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, self-control

qa.q: What is Paul's central theme in Ephesians?
qa.a: Unity of the church as the body of Christ

qa.q: What is the 'armor of God'?
qa.a: Spiritual protection described in Ephesians 6 (truth, righteousness, gospel, faith, salvation, word of God)

qa.q: What does Paul teach about marriage?
qa.a: Wives and husbands should submit to and love each other as Christ loved the church

qa.q: What is Paul's prayer in Ephesians 3?
qa.a: That believers understand the love of Christ and be filled with God's fullness

qa.q: What is the church?
qa.a: The body of Christ, with Christ as head and believers as members

qa.q: Where did Paul write Philippians from?
qa.a: From prison (likely Rome)

qa.q: What is Paul's main theme?
qa.a: Joy and unity in Christ despite persecution

qa.q: What is the 'Christ hymn' in Philippians 2?
qa.a: A passage describing Christ's humiliation and exaltation

qa.q: What does Paul say about anxiety?
qa.a: 'Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation pray with thanksgiving'

qa.q: What does Paul mean by 'pressing on'?
qa.a: Continuing to pursue Christ and the goal of eternal life

qa.q: What heresy was Paul addressing in Colossians?
qa.a: False teachings that added requirements to faith in Christ

qa.q: Who is Christ according to Colossians?
qa.a: The head of the church and supreme over all creation

qa.q: What is 'set your minds on things above'?
qa.a: Paul's call to focus on heavenly values rather than earthly concerns

qa.q: What is the household code in Colossians?
qa.a: Instructions for wives, husbands, children, servants, and masters

qa.q: What does Paul emphasize about rules?
qa.a: Rules about food and festivals are shadows of Christ, not essential for salvation

qa.q: What is 1 Thessalonians about?
qa.a: Encouragement to the young church in Thessalonica and teaching on Christ's return

qa.q: What does Paul teach about the Second Coming?
qa.a: Christ will return suddenly, believers should be watchful and holy

qa.q: What is the 'rapture'?
qa.a: The belief that believers will be caught up to meet Christ in the air

qa.q: How should believers live while waiting for Christ?
qa.a: Work diligently, love one another, and live peacefully

qa.q: What encouragement does Paul give about death?
qa.a: Believers need not grieve like those without hope; they will see the Lord

qa.q: Why did Paul write 2 Thessalonians?
qa.a: To clarify false teachings about Christ's return and encourage the persecuted church

qa.q: What does Paul say about 'the lawless one'?
qa.a: A prophecy of a future figure of rebellion (often called the Antichrist)

qa.q: What should believers do while waiting for Christ?
qa.a: Work hard and not be idle or dependent on others

qa.q: What is Paul's command about idleness?
qa.a: 'If a man will not work, he shall not eat'

qa.q: What is Paul's closing prayer?
qa.a: That God would direct their hearts into God's love and Christ's perseverance

qa.q: Who was Timothy?
qa.a: A young pastor and companion of Paul

qa.q: What is Paul's main purpose in 1 Timothy?
qa.a: To instruct Timothy on church leadership and sound doctrine

qa.q: What does Paul teach about elders?
qa.a: Requirements for church leadership: above reproach, faithful, hospitable, able to teach

qa.q: What is Paul's view on money?
qa.a: 'The love of money is the root of all kinds of evil'

qa.q: What is 'the faith'?
qa.a: The body of Christian teaching and doctrine

qa.q: What is Paul's tone in 2 Timothy?
qa.a: Urgent and personal, written near the end of his life

qa.q: What does Paul urge Timothy to do?
qa.a: Be strong in Christ, guard the gospel, and continue in faithful teaching

qa.q: What is Paul's final charge?
qa.a: 'Preach the Word; be ready in season and out of season'

qa.q: What does Paul say about Scripture?
qa.a: 'All Scripture is God-breathed and is useful for teaching, rebuking, correcting and training'

qa.q: What is Paul's final declaration?
qa.a: 'I have fought a good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith'

qa.q: Who was Titus?
qa.a: Paul's companion and leader of the church in Crete

qa.q: What qualifications does Paul list for elders?
qa.a: Blameless, faithful, hospitable, sound in teaching

qa.q: What is Paul's emphasis on good works?
qa.a: Believers should be devoted to good works and set an example

qa.q: What does Paul teach about church discipline?
qa.a: Leaders should rebuke divisive people and false teachers

qa.q: What is Paul's focus on relationships?
qa.a: How different groups (older, younger, slaves) should relate to each other in love

qa.q: What is Philemon about?
qa.a: Paul's letter requesting forgiveness for a runaway slave named Onesimus

qa.q: Who was Onesimus?
qa.a: A slave who ran away from Philemon but became a Christian

qa.q: What does Paul ask Philemon to do?
qa.a: Receive Onesimus as a brother in Christ, not as a slave

qa.q: What is the main theme?
qa.a: Reconciliation and the transformative power of Christ

qa.q: Why is Philemon significant?
qa.a: It shows early Christian teaching on slavery and human dignity in Christ

qa.q: What is Hebrews' main theme?
qa.a: Jesus is superior to all and the ultimate high priest

qa.q: What is the 'better covenant'?
qa.a: Christ's new covenant replacing the old Jewish law

qa.q: Who is the 'author of our salvation'?
qa.a: Jesus Christ, who was perfected through suffering

qa.q: What is faith according to Hebrews 11?
qa.a: 'Being sure of what we hope for and certain of what we do not see'

qa.q: What does Hebrews say about Jesus's priesthood?
qa.a: Jesus is a priest forever in the order of Melchizedek, not based on genealogy

qa.q: What is James's main emphasis?
qa.a: Faith without works is dead; believers must live out their faith

qa.q: What is the 'royal law'?
qa.a: 'Love your neighbor as yourself'

qa.q: What does James teach about the tongue?
qa.a: It is small but powerful; it can bring blessing or curse

qa.q: What is James's teaching on trials?
qa.a: Trials develop perseverance and should be considered joy

qa.q: What does James say about prayer?
qa.a: 'The prayer of a righteous man is powerful and effective'

qa.q: Who was writing to and why?
qa.a: Peter wrote to persecuted Christians to encourage them in their faith

qa.q: What is Peter's main message?
qa.a: Believers should hope in Christ despite suffering

qa.q: What is the 'living stone'?
qa.a: Jesus Christ, the foundation of the church

qa.q: What does Peter teach about submission?
qa.a: Christians should submit to government, masters, and spouses for Christ's sake

qa.q: What is Peter's call to holiness?
qa.a: 'Be holy because I am holy'

qa.q: What false teachings did Peter address?
qa.a: Denial of Christ's return and immoral behavior

qa.q: What does Peter say about false prophets?
qa.a: They will infiltrate the church and lead believers astray

qa.q: What is Peter's message about Christ's return?
qa.a: It is certain but delayed to allow time for repentance

qa.q: What does Peter encourage about Scripture?
qa.a: All Scripture is given by inspiration of the Holy Spirit

qa.q: What is Peter's final exhortation?
qa.a: Grow in grace and knowledge of Jesus Christ

qa.q: What is John's main theme?
qa.a: God is light and love; believers should live in fellowship with God

qa.q: What is the mark of a true Christian?
qa.a: Love for other believers and obedience to God's commands

qa.q: What false teaching did John combat?
qa.a: Gnosticism, which denied Christ came in the flesh

qa.q: What is assurance of salvation?
qa.a: Believers can have confidence they have eternal life through Christ

qa.q: What is 1 John 1:9 about?
qa.a: 'If we confess our sins, He is faithful to forgive us'

qa.q: What is 2 John about?
qa.a: A short letter warning against false teachers and emphasizing love

qa.q: Who is the 'chosen lady'?
qa.a: Likely either a specific woman or a church personified as a woman

qa.q: What does John command?
qa.a: Love one another and do not welcome false teachers

qa.q: What are the 'antichrists'?
qa.a: Those who deny Jesus came in the flesh

qa.q: What is John's closing greeting?
qa.a: 'The children of your sister send their greetings'

qa.q: What is 3 John about?
qa.a: John's commendation of Gaius for his hospitality and concern about Diotrephes

qa.q: Who was Gaius?
qa.a: A believer praised for his support of missionaries

qa.q: Who was Diotrephes?
qa.a: A church leader who rejected John's authority and refused hospitality

qa.q: What does John advocate?
qa.a: Support for missionaries and traveling evangelists

qa.q: What is John's prayer for Gaius?
qa.a: That he prosper in health as his soul prospers

qa.q: What is Jude's main purpose?
qa.a: To warn believers against false teachers and ungodly behavior

qa.q: What examples does Jude give of judgment?
qa.a: Fallen angels, Sodom and Gomorrah, and Korah's rebellion

qa.q: What are the characteristics of false teachers?
qa.a: They pervert grace into licentiousness and deny Jesus as Lord

qa.q: What should believers do?
qa.a: Build themselves up in faith, pray, and keep themselves in God's love

qa.q: What is Jude's closing doxology?
qa.a: Glory to God who is able to keep believers from stumbling

qa.q: What is Revelation about?
qa.a: Visions of the end times and Christ's ultimate victory over evil

qa.q: Who is the 'Lamb' in Revelation?
qa.a: Jesus Christ, the slain Lamb who takes away sins

qa.q: What are the 'seven seals'?
qa.a: Seals on a scroll representing judgments in the end times

qa.q: What is the 'New Jerusalem'?
qa.a: The holy city described as the dwelling place of God with His people

qa.q: What is John's final message?
qa.a: 'Behold, I am coming soon!' And believers should respond 'Amen. Come, Lord Jesus'
